questions.text: I rarely pick up on the implicit rules of a new social group without being told.
I believe that if I have something to give, I should give it.
I tend to set the pace in group work rather than follow it.
I hold the view that the person most responsible for my well-being is me.
I hold the view that some of the most important discoveries come from wandering without a clear plan.
I tend to pick the option that strengthens my personal position, even when alternatives would benefit the group more.
I find that celebrations and exciting events feel pleasant without tipping into overwhelm.
I am known for bridging differences in group deliberations.
I sense when a seemingly casual question is actually loaded with significance.
I regularly accommodate the prevailing direction to maintain group flow.
I rarely sense when the emotional tone of a text conversation shifts.
I think that approaching painful situations with calm objectivity is a form of respect for the complexity of what people are going through.
I feel that the weight I give to suffering in my ethical thinking is the quality that has most clearly shaped my sense of what a life well lived actually looks like.
I find that when I process others' pain through an objective lens, I am less likely to make decisions that favor the dramatic over the important.
I often feel emotionally wrung out by the end of the day.
I thrive when I can respond to whatever the moment presents rather than sticking to a predetermined path.
I find that I rarely need coping strategies because my emotional system self-regulates efficiently.
I set reminders and alarms to keep myself on track throughout the day.
